title: Xenial Molten Croatian Mushroom Skillet
description: A rich and savory Croatian dish featuring molten mushrooms and a hint of umami, perfect for a date night, this recipe is also Buddhist-temple-friendly as it does not contain any animal by-products or alcohol, and it utilizes unflavored gelatin.
instructions: Pressure cook the vegetable broth and gelatin in a separate pot until the gelatin is dissolved.
Heat the olive oil in a cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Add the mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour the gelatin mixture over the mushrooms in the skillet.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 15 minutes or until the mixture is molten and bubbly.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
